title: Fraction Division Problem Solving
why: Word problems with fraction division ask "how many pieces fit?" or "how big is each share?" To answer, you need equal-group thinking and the flip-and-multiply move warm. Get those fluent and the words turn into a plan.

diagnostic.q: A ribbon is shared into pieces of 1/2 ft each. How many pieces come from 2 ft?

learn.heading: Turn words into a division plan
learn.intro: When a problem asks "how many small pieces fit?" it is a division. Count how many unit fractions fill the whole amount, or use flip-and-multiply. Read carefully to see what is being shared.
examples: How many fit: "How many 1/4 cups in 2 cups?" → each cup holds 4 fourths, so 2 × 4 = 8.

tiers.intro: Count the pieces one whole at a time.

items.q: A. How many 1/2 cups are in 1 cup?
B. So how many 1/2 cups are in 2 cups? (2 × 2)
C. How many 1/4 cups are in 1 cup?

tiers.intro: Read the story, then count or multiply.

items.q: A. A board is 3 ft long, cut into 1/2-ft pieces. How many pieces? (3 × 2)
items.hint: Each foot gives 2 half-foot pieces, so 3 × 2 = 6.

items.q: B. You have 2 cups of rice scooped 1/3 cup at a time. How many scoops?
items.hint: Each cup gives 3 thirds, so 2 × 3 = 6 scoops.

items.q: A. 1 ÷ 1/4: how many 1/4 pieces fit in 1 whole?
B. A 1/2-cup of nuts is split into 1/4-cup bags. 1/2 ÷ 1/4 = how many bags?

exit.q: 1. A 4-ft rope is cut into 1/2-ft pieces. How many pieces? (4 × 2)
exit.hint: Each foot gives 2 half-foot pieces, so 4 × 2 = 8.

exit.q: 2. 1 ÷ 1/3: how many 1/3 cups fit in 1 cup?
